title: Tree-Top Triumph: A Daring Stand for Nature
story: Julia Butterfly Hill's feet finally touched the ground on December 18, 1999. She had just spent over two years living high up in a giant redwood tree! Hill climbed the ancient tree, which she named Luna, to protect it from being cut down. Her peaceful protest in the forests of Humboldt County lasted an incredible 738 days. Her dedication helped save Luna and raised awareness about protecting old-growth forests.

title: Cinderella's Slipper: A Royal Fashion Joke?
story: A Scottish professor made a surprising discovery about Cinderella's famous glass slipper in 2022. Genevieve Warwick found that the magical footwear might actually be a clever joke! She believes the story's author, Charles Perrault, was playfully teasing King Louis XIV of France. The king loved fancy shoes and built a huge Hall of Mirrors at his palace. So, Perrault combined these royal obsessions into one sparkly shoe.

story: Stumps flew and cricket balls soared as Zimbabwe and England faced off for the first time in a Test match. On December 18, 1996, these two nations clashed on the cricket pitch, marking a historic moment in the sport. Zimbabwe, a rising star in international cricket, proved they could compete with the best. The match showcased skill, strategy, and sportsmanship, thrilling fans from both countries.

title: Flames Engulf Stilt Homes in Brazil
story: Flames leapt from house to house in Manaus, Brazil. On December 18, 2018, a devastating fire swept through a neighborhood of stilt homes. These unique dwellings, perched above water on wooden poles, were no match for the blaze. Firefighters battled the inferno, but many families lost everything. The community rallied, offering shelter and support to those affected.
activity_question: What type of homes were affected by the fire in Manaus?
follow_up_question: Why might people build homes on stilts in some areas?

title: Covax: A Global Vaccine Sharing Plan
story: In 2020, a superhero team for global health emerged. Covax, an international group, launched a plan to share COVID-19 vaccines fairly. On December 18, they announced deals with vaccine makers and a way for countries to share extra doses. This meant more people worldwide could get protected, especially in places with less money for vaccines.
activity_question: What did Covax announce on December 18, 2020?
follow_up_question: Why was Covax's plan important for countries with less money?

title: A Feast of Victory
story: Cheers erupted across America in 1777! The young nation celebrated its first national Thanksgiving Day on December 18. This special feast honored a big win in the Revolutionary War. British General Burgoyne had surrendered, boosting American spirits. Families gathered to share food and gratitude for their growing freedom.
activity_question: What event did the first national Thanksgiving Day commemorate?
activity_choices: The signing of the Declaration of Independence
follow_up_question: How might this Thanksgiving have been different from modern celebrations?
